She made a promise to her husband to keep their tree farm going

Timothy Castagne sabe que Bélgica debe jugar su mejor partido ante Estados Unidos
items.summary: El lateral derecho de Bélgica aseguró que los temas extra cancha que han rodeado este partido han pasado desapercibidos para los jugadores, ya que ellos están concentrados en dar lo mejor en el campo y conseguir el resultado.

items.title: Balogun starts for US against Belgium after suspension lifted
items.summary: Folarin Balogun is in the United States’ starting lineup for a World Cup round of 16 match against Belgium after his red-card suspension was lifted by FIFA in a decision that sparked an uproar across the sport

items.title: Pulisic y Estados Unidos llegan a Seattle con el objetivo de avanzar a cuartos de final
items.summary: Estados Unidos es el único anfitrión que queda vivo en la competición, Pulisic y compañía quieren llevar al Team USA a lo más alto, pero Bélgica quiere dar el golpe en la mesa con Kevin De Bruyne y Romelu Lukaku.

items.title: Roberto Martínez le dice adiós a Portugal: "Es mi último juego con la selección"
items.summary: En la conferencia de prensa posterior al juego en el que España eliminó a Portugal del Mundial, su entrenador, Roberto Martínez, anunció de manera oficial su adiós al cargo de la selección masculina afirmando que se lleva el cariño de los portugueses.

items.title: Unai Simón sobre el gol: “Creo que habla bien de la concentración de este equipo en los 90 minutos”
items.summary: El guardameta español destaca el trabajo de equipo al saber leer al oponente y responder con cambio de juego y buen entendimiento del juego por parte de todos los jugadores, tanto titulares como cambios.

items.title: Family of man shot by Tennessee National Guard demands release of video
items.summary: The grandfather of a man shot and killed by two members of the Tennessee National Guard in Memphis says he wants answers

items.title: Marc Cucurella: “Sabíamos que iba haber momentos sufridos, hemos merecido la victoria”
items.summary: El defensor español se muestra con gran ánimo tras el valioso triunfo sobre Portugal. Comenta las buenas sensaciones de la Furia Roja y el progreso del equipo en la Copa Mundial.

items.title: Bedoya: “Este partido va a ser muy diferente al que jugó en Atlanta” | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: El exjugador de la selección de las ‘barras y las estrellas’ comenta que la inclusión de dos nuevos jugadores al equipo que se enfrentó previamente a Bélgica puede marcar la diferencia y cree que Estados Unidos llegará con mayor confianza.

items.title: DOJ defends decision to drop criminal charges against billionaire Indian businessman
items.summary: The government says its decision to dismiss its case against Gautam Adani, one of the world’s wealthiest people, wasn’t swayed by his reported offer to invest in the U.S.

items.title: Altidore: “A mí no me gusta como reacciona UEFA” | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: El exjugador Jozy Altidore cree que Estados Unidos tiene grandes posibilidades de pasar a cuartos de final. Sobre la polémica de la tarjeta roja a Balogun, Altidore no está de acuerdo con la decisión de la tarjeta condonada.

items.title: Nuno Gomes: “Portugal no arriesgó y lo pagó”
items.summary: Con la decepción en el rostro, nuestro experto puso el dedo en la llaga y acusó al equipo luso de no buscar el triunfo en los 90 minutos y eso le costó muy caro.

items.title: Mikel Merino: “Estoy disfrutando de unos momentos más felices en mi carrera”
items.summary: El mediocampista del Arsenal se pone la capa de héroe al marcar el gol de la victoria sobre Portugal, otorgando a España el pase a los cuartos de final en la Copa Mundial. Se muestra feliz e inspirado por el gran momento en su carrera.

items.title: El abrazo de Lamine Yamal a Cristiano Ronaldo tras la eliminación de Portugal en el Mundial
items.summary: El joven español protagonizó un emotivo gesto con Cristiano Ronaldo durante su último partido en Copas del Mundo.

items.title: Charlie Kirk's family attends key preliminary hearing for suspect charged with his murder
items.summary: A five-day hearing began Monday in Utah to determine if alleged Charlie Kirk shooter Tyler Robinson will stand trial for murder. NBC News' Camila Bernal reports on the evidence presented and Erika Kirk's courtroom attendance.

items.title: National Guard soldiers fatally shoot man who had gun in downtown Memphis
items.summary: Police said the man turned his gun on the Tennessee National Guard members, who were deployed to the city by a Trump administration order last year.

items.title: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner denies new allegations of sexual assault
items.summary: Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner is denying a new allegation of sexual assault, an explosive development injecting tumult into a race that is central to the Democratic Party’s path to winning back the Senate. Despite the denial, Platner said in a video that he is taking “time to reflect on the best path forward” with his candidacy.

items.title: This World Cup marks end of era for Ronaldo and other soccer greats
items.summary: Some of the greatest footballers of a generation played their final World Cup match this summer.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo llora tras ser eliminado en su último Mundial
items.summary: Un gol sobre el final del partido sentenció el partido ante España en los Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo 2026. Estas son las imágenes emotivas del astro portugués.

items.title: USA set to take on Belgium in World Cup amid Trump red card controversy
items.summary: Ahead of this evening's World Cup match between the U.S. and Belgium, the drama about the eligibility of U.S. striker Folarin Balogun continues to swirl following his red card in the previous match.

items.title: Lauren Bennett, singer on ‘Party Rock Anthem,’ dies at 37
items.summary: “Our hearts are broken,” said Bennett’s former musical group, G.R.L. Bennett was featured on LMFAO’s 2011 hit.

items.title: Venezuela’s earthquake death toll reaches 3,535 as survivors look for missing relatives
items.summary: As the number of deaths increased Monday, survivors like 12-year-old Fabiana Blanco say they have been left with “a battle scar.”

items.title: Graham Platner considering ‘best path forward’ in Maine Senate race after denying sexual assault claim
items.summary: Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner is denying a new allegation of sexual assault, an explosive development injecting tumult into a key Senate race.

items.title: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' access to app stores for the time being
items.summary: Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.

items.title: Graham Platner faces growing calls to withdraw following allegation of sexual assault
items.summary: The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."

items.title: Supreme Court won't block state from enforcing age verification law for app downloads
items.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has declined to block Texas from enforcing a state law that requires age verification and parental consent for users seeking to download apps or make in-app purchases on mobile phones

items.title: Former college basketball player Kerr Kriisa accused of fraud in $2.2M scheme
items.summary: Former college basketball player Kerr Kriisa has been accused of fraudulently obtaining nearly $2.2 million from two victims

items.title: BJ’s roof collapses during heavy rain in New Jersey
items.summary: Heavy rain pounded New Jersey on Monday and caused the roof over a big box store to partially collapse, temporarily trapping two people inside, authorities said.

items.title: Rebounds for AI stocks lift US indexes
items.summary: A rebound for AI stocks is helping to support Wall Street and keeping indexes mixed

items.title: England’s Jordan Henderson seriously injured during ‘Wonderwall’ celebration after World Cup win
items.summary: The Brentford midfielder had to be taken off the pitch on a stretcher after he jumped over a short wall and landed awkwardly.

items.title: Man sues DHS after agents tracked him down for sending a scathing email to ICE
items.summary: Federal agents tried to track David Streever to his home and hotel, and left him a warning notice that a critical email he sent the former head of ICE may have been illegal. Now he's suing.

items.title: Mourners throng funeral procession in Tehran for Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
items.summary: Mourners dressed in black have flooded into Iran’s capital for a procession as part of the funeral of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

items.title: 'That wasn't a foul': Trump shares details of call with FIFA president
items.summary: President Trump discussed his call with FIFA President Gianni Infantino over Folarin Balogun's red card in the World Cup game against Bosnia and Herzegovina and his request to review the penalty.

items.title: Militant LGBTQ+ rights group 'the Lavender Panthers' was founded on this day in 1973
items.summary: "Reverend Ray" Broshears founded the queer vigilante group the Lavender Panthers in 1973. The group's impact is still felt today.

items.title: Historic World Cup furor at 'incomprehensible' FIFA decision to let U.S. forward Balogun play
items.summary: The Belgian soccer federation is challenging FIFA's ruling to let Balogun play despite getting a red card in his previous game. Belgium's statement came just 11 hours before kickoff in Seattle. European soccer body UEFA criticizes FIFA's "incomprehensible and unjustifiable decision."

items.title: Ohio police officer fatally shot responding to 911 gunshots call, suspect and 2 others also dead
items.summary: A police officer responding to a 911 call about gunshots at an Ohio home has been fatally shot

items.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Guests Spill Wedding Secrets
items.summary: Some of the guests of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Madison Square Garden wedding ceremony are sharing a glimpse of what it was like inside the star-studded event. Exclusive details reveal more about the couple’s “emotional” wedding vows and describe how the two turned the venue into a “secret garden” that showcases key moments throughout their lives and relationship. NBC’s Emilie Ikeda reports for TODAY.

items.title: Trump, Mamdani give contrasting July 4th speeches. And, U.S. faces Belgium in World Cup
items.summary: For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Prosecutors to lay out their case against the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
items.summary: Prosecutors will present their case this week against the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk

items.title: Hate food waste? 7 creative ways to turn your leftovers into a new meal
items.summary: We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.

items.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
items.summary: Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.

items.title: Russia launches deadly wave of missiles and drones on Kyiv
items.summary: A Russian missile and drone attack left at least seven people dead hours after Ukrainian President Zelenskyy warned of an imminent attack on Kyiv. The latest large-scale attack comes says after Russian missile and drone strikes killed at least 31 people in the Ukrainian capital.

items.title: 3 firefighters killed in Colorado remembered for their bravery as wildfires churn in the West
items.summary: Wildland firefighters have gathered to pay tribute to three of their own who died after they were trapped by flames a week ago in western Colorado

items.title: A new ICE facility could speed up deportations for families and kids
items.summary: The Trump administration plans to open a 528-bed holding center for migrant families and unaccompanied children awaiting removal from the U.S., putting it next to a Louisiana airfield that has become the nation’s largest hub of deportation flights

items.title: China fires a missile from a submarine in a test launch in the South Pacific, state media say
items.summary: China fires a missile from a submarine in a test launch in the South Pacific, state media say

items.title: England hands Mexico its first World Cup loss at Estadio Azteca, winning 3-2 to reach quarterfinals
items.summary: England hands Mexico its first World Cup loss at Estadio Azteca, winning 3-2 to reach quarterfinals

items.title: Fierce winds hit US Pacific territories as Super Typhoon Bavi nears Guam
items.summary: U.S. territories in the western Pacific prepare for storm's landfall Monday.

items.title: National Guard members on patrol in Memphis fatally shoot man during pursuit, police say
items.summary: Tennessee National Guard members assigned by the Trump administration to a crime-fighting patrol in Memphis fatally shot a man who authorities say turned toward the soldiers with a gun during a downtown pursuit

items.title: 7 OPEC+ countries agree to expand monthly oil production modestly as prices slide
items.summary: A handful of countries in the OPEC+ oil-producing alliance are planning to increase their outputs modestly next month

items.title: Trump to meet with Ukraine's Zelenskyy and Syria's al-Sharaa during the NATO summit
items.summary: President Donald Trump plans to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa while attending the upcoming NATO summit in Turkey

items.title: Delta pilot reports a close encounter with a July Fourth firework before landing in Chicago
items.summary: A Delta Air Lines flight and a high-flying firework reportedly came into contact in the sky above Chicago as the city celebrated the Fourth of July holiday

items.title: Democrat Mallory McMorrow suspends her Michigan Senate campaign and scrambles the pivotal race
items.summary: Michigan Democrat Mallory McMorrow has suspended her campaign for the U.S. Senate

items.title: FIFA lifts US player Balogun’s red card suspension at World Cup after Trump calls Infantino
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump intervened on behalf of American star forward Folarin Balogun, whose suspension was lifted in a decision that allows him to play in a World Cup match against Belgium on Monday

items.title: Fire breaks out on Brooklyn Bridge during New York's fireworks show
items.summary: A fire broke out on the Brooklyn Bridge in New York during the city’s July Fourth fireworks show

items.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
items.summary: A pastor of a prominent underground church detained in China in October has been released, according to rights advocates

items.title: A timeline of Charlie Kirk's assassination and the arrest of Tyler Robinson
items.summary: The 23-year-old man accused of killing Charlie Kirk will be in court this week for the case's most substantial hearing so far

items.title: 150 people from 50 countries become US citizens at Mount Vernon on America's 250th birthday
items.summary: A group of 150 people from 50 countries has become U.S. citizens at George Washington's Mount Vernon estate

items.title: Prosecutors argue the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
items.summary: Prosecutors in Charlie Kirk's murder case will aim to convince a judge they have enough evidence to try his accused killer and seek the death penalty

items.title: Female American rower completes record-breaking solo journey
items.summary: A Grand Canyon river rafting guide who aimed to become the first American woman to row across the mid-Pacific solo has completed a record-breaking journey from California to Hawaii

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
